sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च। शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च॥
transliteration: brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāham amṛtasyāvyayasya ca | śāśvatasya ca dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya ca ||
literal_meaning: I am the foundation of the imperishable, immortal, eternal Brahman, of the ultimate happiness and of the unchanging Dharma.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that the highest impersonal light (Brahman) and the deepest joy are not separate from the personal Self—both are grounded in the same conscious source, dissolving the split between ‘seeking’ and ‘being’.
psychological_state: Post-insight integration: the mind that has glimpsed transcendence now learns it can safely relax into relationship rather than remain in abstract detachment.
trigger_scenario: After a silent retreat you feel blissfully empty, yet back home you feel disconnected from loved ones; this verse reminds you that the same awareness holds both the silence and the embrace.
anchor_text: Rest in the unchanging source where seeking and being are one.
